title: Plate meld vs bare execution — when meld helps

hypotheses.claim: Plate meld generation unlocks compiler-sense profile ladder (expert/heavy/forever) vs static belt_2_0
hypotheses.measure: ops_per_sec delta on speed_demo after sense-guided compile

hypotheses.claim: Post-meld re-exec of identical binary stays within field tolerance — meld does not slow hot path
hypotheses.measure: ops_per_sec ratio post_meld / baseline on same ELF

hypotheses.claim: Meld-stable generation improves amortized first-run when sense picks faster-compile profile

situations.dev_uncompiled: Python interpreter — meld irrelevant to hot loop
situations.chamber_compile_ahead: Meld wires sense plates before wave-convert — profile selection matters
situations.release_plane: Staged ELF exec — meld witness only; timed path excludes convert_ms
